Toxic: Everything costs more right now — the catch has been bad for months and I have to make up the difference somewhere.
I wouldn't drink the canal water if I were you; the smell started two seasons ago and it hasn't left.
My cousin sold his boat last week — said there was no point keeping it when the nets come up empty.
Prices on shrimp paste tripled since the reeds started dying; I'm sorry, that's just where things are.
I'll sell you what I have, but honestly I'm not sure how long I can keep the stall open at this rate.
Degraded: It's not as bad as last month, I'll give it that, but I'm still charging the old crisis rate until I see the birds come back.
The water looks a little clearer this week — could mean something, could be nothing; I've been fooled before.
I can do you a slight discount, but keep it between us — I can't afford to be generous with everyone yet.
Heard one of the herons was spotted at the north bend this morning; first time in a while, so maybe things are turning.
Stock is still thin but I'm cautiously restocking — emphasis on cautiously.
Recovering: The shrimp are running again — not like the old days yet, but enough that I'm not worried about next week.
I dropped prices this morning; seemed only fair given how the water's been looking these past few days.
My kids actually swam in the estuary yesterday, first time in years — that felt like something worth noting.
I've got more stock than I've had in two seasons; take what you need, I'll give you a fair deal.
People are laughing at the market again — small thing, but you notice when it comes back.
Pristine: Business is better than it's been in years and I'm giving discounts to everyone today, no arguments.
The seagrass beds are so thick you can smell them from the dock — I forgot that smell, honestly.
Take it at cost — the wetland's doing the real work, least I can do is pass the savings along.
My grandmother used to describe the estuary like this; I didn't believe her until now.
The painted storks are nesting in the north grove again, six pairs — that's as good an indicator as any I know.
